channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Governing AI Agents Against Every OWASP Agentic Risk: A Deep Dive with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: AI agents are moving from prototypes to production. They book flights, write code, negotiate contracts, and operate across enterprise systems with minimal human oversight. The attack surface is not theoretical: OWASP has catalogued the top 10 risks specific to agentic applications, and every one of...
item.dc:creator: mosiddi

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.5, which includes accuracy improvements across C/C++,… The post CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Now Generally Available] Customizable Security Baseline Policies in Machine Configuration!
item.description: Background: Azure Machine Configuration remains committed to enabling greater security and simplicity in at-scale server management for all Azure customers. Machine Configuration (previously known as Azure Policy Guest Configuration) enables both built-in and custom configuration as code allowing...
item.dc:creator: mutemwamasheke

item.title: Who's Calling Your Service? Designing for Humans and Agents at the Same Time
item.description: We're building three interfaces for Azure SRE Agent: an interactive CLI for humans at a terminal, an agent mode for coding agents that spawn it as a subprocess, and an MCP server for humans inside coding agents and for remote agents in other ecosystems. The CLI and agent mode are coming. The MCP...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: (Co)work in Progress
item.description: A complete record of milestones, launches, and newest innovations. Today we are again expanding model choice in Microsoft 365 Copilot with the addition of Anthropic's latest model—Claude Opus 4.8—now available in Copilot Cowork (Frontier). Learn more about our announcement here. Give Microsoft 365...
item.dc:creator: ilyagrebnov

item.title: Available today: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8 in Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Today we’re expanding model choice in Microsoft 365 Copilot with the addition of Anthropic’s latest model—Claude Opus 4.8. Available now in Copilot Cowork (Frontier) and rolling out to Copilot Chat, Excel, PowerPoint, and Copilot Studio. Opus 4.8 builds on Opus 4.7 and previous Opus model...
item.dc:creator: Bas Brekelmans

item.title: Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here.
item.description: The ESC collection lets you escape the confines of your desk and get out into the sun where good ideas are bound to happen. The post Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here. appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 is now available in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: As AI applications become more complex, teams need models that can handle longer-running work, reason more deeply across complex analysis use tools more reliably across multi-step workflows, and support consistency for production workflows. Claude Opus 4.8 is designed for these demands, with...
item.dc:creator: amar_badal

item.title: Ansible + Azure Arc: Manage Azure Arc Extensions with New Ansible Modules
item.description: We’re excited to announce new modules in Ansible Galaxy that make it easier to manage Azure Arc machine extensions at scale. With the latest updates to the azure.azcollection on Ansible Galaxy, you can now deploy and manage Azure Arc extensions using familiar, declarative Ansible workflows. These...
item.dc:creator: alinetran

item.title: [Public Preview] Introducing Customizable Security Baseline Policies in Machine Configuration
item.description: Background: Azure Machine Configuration remains committed to enabling greater security and simplicity in at-scale server management for all Azure customers. Machine Configuration (previously known as Azure Policy Guest Configuration) enables both built-in and custom configuration as code allowing...
item.dc:creator: mutemwamasheke

item.title: How Azure Chaos Studio ships with hermetic Aspire end-to-end tests
item.description: Learn how the Azure Chaos Studio team uses Aspire.Hosting.Testing to run hermetic, per-PR end-to-end tests across four services — with emulators, stubs, and no shared environments. The post How Azure Chaos Studio ships with hermetic Aspire end-to-end tests appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Brent Rusinow

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.122
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.122 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8, Anthropic’s latest Opus model, is now available in GitHub Copilot. In our early testing, Opus 4.8 demonstrates a clear step forward in code understanding and generation across… The post Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Gentlemen ransomware: Dissecting a self-propagating Go encryptor
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence presents a comprehensive analysis of The Gentlemen, a Go-based ransomware deployed by affiliates of Storm-2697 that combines per-file ephemeral key encryption with an aggressive self-propagation module to deploy itself across an entire network using series of...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Finance Agent Benchmark: evaluating and improving AI for Finance
item.description: As business complexity accelerates, finance organizations are being asked to support more stakeholders, guide more decisions, and operate at unprecedented speed, often without additional resources. As these demands outpace traditional tools and processes, AI presents an opportunity to reshape how...
item.dc:creator: Sigita_Cepaitiene

item.title: SQL + AI, hands-on: Join a free workshop near you
item.description: If you work with Microsoft SQL regularly, the AI conversation right now probably feels a little exhausting. Every week brings a new platform, a new pattern, a new opinion about how you’re “supposed” to build AI apps. Most of it assumes you’ll start over. You don’t have to. We’re running SQL AI App...
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman

item.title: Building Agentic Systems on Azure: Microsoft Foundry Agents SDK vs Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: In my recent experience as a Senior Consultant at Microsoft, I’ve been actively involved in designing and delivering AI-driven solutions, with a strong focus on building intelligent agents using modern frameworks. Along the way, I've built agents using both Microsoft Foundry Agents SDK (hereafter...
item.dc:creator: ChaitanyaThalloory

item.title: Verification-driven tooling prompts for fast-moving codebases
item.description: A practical pattern for using GenAI research agents to produce reliable internal tooling by verifying assumptions with detection classes and a final checklist. The post Verification-driven tooling prompts for fast-moving codebases appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Dexter Williams

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Application Gateway for Containers – Service Mesh integration with Istio
item.description: Application Gateway for Containers service mesh integration with Istio is now generally available. This capability simplifies secure north-south traffic into workloads running in an Istio service mesh by automating mutual TLS (mTLS) connectivity between A
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Access Your SRE Agent from Any IDE, Terminal, or AI Assistant
item.description: Your team already uses an SRE Agent — it monitors your services, learns your architecture, and handles operational tasks. Now developers can talk to that agent in natural language from the interfaces they already use every day: their editor, their terminal, their AI assistant. Check what the agent...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: I’m Starting a New Cosmos DB App. What Security Do I Actually Need?
item.description: You just created a Cosmos DB account. The portal handed you two keys and a connection string, it worked, and you moved on. That’s what most developers do, and it causes problems later. This post is a guide for developers launching a new Cosmos DB app who want a secure default setup without...
item.dc:creator: Sudhanshu Khera, Iria Osara

item.title: Inspektor Gadget Completes Its First Independent Security Audit
item.description: One thing I've learned working with Open Source software over the years is that the projects you can trust most are the ones willing to let someone else test and review. That's what's recently happened with Inspektor Gadget, the open source eBPF tool for Kubernetes observability and Linux host...
item.dc:creator: Brian Benz

item.title: How AI coding agents actually use your technology
item.description: You ship an SDK, a CLI, an API, and developers use it. Now AI coding agents use it too, except they use it differently than humans do. Most of the time you have no idea what’s actually happening between “developer types a prompt” and “agent generates code with your technology.” Is the agent reading...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Multiparty private offers in Microsoft Marketplace expand to 30 countries in Europe
item.description: Multiparty private offers give Microsoft partners a way to procure third-party cloud and AI solutions for customers through Marketplace. Software companies can expand into new markets by working with partners that bring established customer relationships,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Recertified: How Microsoft 365 Copilot continues to earn trust and What Changed in a Year
item.description: Recertification: ISO 42001 for the second consecutive year Today, we are announcing that Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat (Copilot) have achieved ISO/IEC 42001:2023 recertification independently validated for the second consecutive year. First certified in March 2025, Copilot was among the...
item.dc:creator: Oliver_Bell

item.title: Introducing Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro Model Context Protocol Tools on VS Code
item.description: About Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro MCP Tools The Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro MCP Tools introduces a natural language-driven interface that simplifies how users interact with geospatial data managed with Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro. By integrating directly with GitHub Copilot in VS...
item.dc:creator: nelsontam

item.title: v1.15.5
item.description: 1.15.5 (May 27, 2026) ENHANCEMENTS: Support for module version evaluating to null (in the context of dynamic module sources) (#38632) BUG FIXES: Fix crash on init for modules with empty source (#38628)
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Scaling the reach of finance: what’s next with Finance Agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: In most organizations, finance is a small but mighty strategic partner to the business, supporting enterprise-wide decisions while managing core processes from record-to-report and source-to-pay to forecast-to-plan. The insights and guidance finance teams produce are more than just outputs of these...
item.dc:creator: April_Olson

item.title: Multi-Tenant Architecture: Real Challenges and an Azure Design Walkthrough
item.description: Azure Multi-Tenant Architecture (B2C Scenario) Let’s start with a reference design commonly used in Azure-based systems. A pretty standard setup looks something like this: Microsoft Entra External ID (Azure AD B2C) for authentication Azure API Management as the entry layer App Service or Functions...
item.dc:creator: pranav_pratik

item.title: From poisoned search results to GPU mining: A cryptojacking campaign abusing ScreenConnect and Microsoft .NET utilities
item.description: Microsoft exposes a cryptojacking campaign using SEO poisoning and ScreenConnect to target high-performance PCs, with malicious sites also surfaced through AI chatbots. The post From poisoned search results to GPU mining: A cryptojacking campaign abusing ScreenConnect and Microsoft .NET utilities...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Experts and Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Copilot Memory has more controls for deletion, scope, and the Copilot CLI
item.description: Copilot Memory now includes improved memory deletion, adds a repository-level off switch, and brings further memory controls into the Copilot CLI. Copilot Memory is in public preview and available to… The post Copilot Memory has more controls for deletion, scope, and the Copilot CLI appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Simplify Virtual WAN Spoke Connectivity at Scale with Azure Virtual Network Manager
item.description: With Azure Virtual Network Manager (AVNM) integration, organizations using Virtual WAN for transitive connectivity can simplify spoke connectivity and policy management across large-scale hub-and-spoke deployments. By using a Virtual WAN hub as the hub in an AVNM hub-and-spoke topology,...
item.dc:creator: Jay-Li

item.title: GitHub Code Quality: Repository Enablement API
item.description: You can now programmatically enable and configure GitHub Code Quality on individual repositories using the new Repository Enablement API, available today in public preview. Two new endpoints are now available:… The post GitHub Code Quality: Repository Enablement API appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules
item.description: Enterprise owners now have granular control over which GitHub Copilot models are available to each organization. With targeted model rules, you can allow specific models for specific organizations instead of… The post Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: How ACR Runs Multi-Tenancy at Scale: Stamp Rebalancing and Why You Never See It Happen
item.description: By Johnson Shi, Richard Yuan, Yi Zha, Susan Shi, Jeanine Burke, Bin Du, Clark Porter, Bernie Harris, Eric Du Introduction Two of the most common questions we hear from teams running container workloads at scale on Azure Container Registry (ACR) are: "How does ACR keep my registry's performance...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Virtual Network Manager integration with Virtual WAN
item.description: Azure Virtual Network Manager integration with Virtual WAN is now in public preview, enabling you to use an Azure Virtual WAN hub as the hub in Azure Virtual Network Manager hub-and-spoke connectivity configurations, bringing together Azure Virtual Networ
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Reconciling the Past: Correcting Records for Unfixed Kubernetes CVEs
item.description: The Kubernetes project relies on transparency to empower cluster administrators and security researchers. One important way we do that is by publishing CVE records into the Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures database. As part of our ongoing effort to mature the official Kubernetes CVE Feed, we...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Doing More with GitHub Copilot as a .NET Developer
item.description: Want to get more out of your GitHub Copilot experience? Here are some easy ways to get started. The post Doing More with GitHub Copilot as a .NET Developer appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Wendy Breiding (SHE/HER)

item.title: Give Your .NET MAUI Android Apps a Material 3 Makeover
item.description: Material 3 (a.k.a. Material You) support is now available for .NET MAUI apps on Android. Here is how to enable it, what changes today, and what is still on the way. The post Give Your .NET MAUI Android Apps a Material 3 Makeover appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Versluis

item.title: What’s New in Notebooks | May 2026
item.description: Copilot Notebooks gets smarter for Microsoft 365 Copilot users Work rarely lives in one place. A single project can span a deck in SharePoint, decisions in a Teams thread, customer context in email, and ideas captured in quick conversations. Copilot Notebooks brings that work together in a shared,...
item.dc:creator: ezrapark

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Virtual network flow logs connector with Microsoft Sentinel
item.description: Azure virtual network flow logs connector with Microsoft Sentinel is now generally available, enabling you to seamlessly export and analyze network traffic data within your security operations workflows. With this integration, you can bring rich, network-
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem
item.description: Dependabot now supports sbt. Add sbt as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml file. Dependabot will then monitor your build.sbt inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are available… The post Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Announcing general availability of Azure Intel® TDX confidential VMs
item.description: We’re excited to announce the general availability of Azure’s next generation of confidential virtual machines, powered by 5th Gen Intel® Xeon® processors with Intel® Trust Domain Extensions (Intel® TDX). These new confidential VMs make it easier than ever for organizations to move their most...
item.dc:creator: simranparkhe

item.title: Filter secret scanning approval requests by sort order and bypass status
item.description: This week, we’re rolling out two improvements to our delegated workflows for secret scanning. What’s changing Sort bypass and dismissal requests in the UI: You can now choose between ascending… The post Filter secret scanning approval requests by sort order and bypass status appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Classroom sign-ups are no longer available
item.description: Starting today, new sign-ups for GitHub Classroom are no longer available as we transition to partner solutions. If you already have a GitHub Classroom account or existing classrooms, you can… The post GitHub Classroom sign-ups are no longer available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Visual Studio May Update – Plan, Review, Refine
item.description: There’s a particular rhythm to good development work: you think, you try, you check, you adjust. This month’s Visual Studio update leans into that rhythm. Whether you’re sketching an approach with the Plan agent before touching a single file, reviewing a wave of changes across many files, or...
item.dc:creator: Mark Downie

item.title: Code coverage on pull requests is now in public preview
item.description: Code coverage metrics are now in public preview for all GitHub Code Quality users on github.com. You can now see an aggregate percent of code covered directly on pull requests,… The post Code coverage on pull requests is now in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Building an On-Device Voice Assistant with Microsoft Foundry Local
item.description: Why on-device voice still matters Most "voice AI" tutorials assume your audio leaves the machine. You ship a WAV to Whisper-API, your transcript to GPT-4, and a synthesized response back over the wire. That works — but it also means three round trips, three per-token bills, and three places your...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with Git and GitHub in VS Code
item.description: Discover how to use VS Code to interact with GitHub and maintain your projects. The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with Git and GitHub in VS Code appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Deploying Existing Agentic Services to Microsoft 365 Copilot via Gateway and OBO
item.description: Deploying an Agentic Service to Microsoft 365 Copilot with Delegated OBO Access You've built an agentic service. It works. It uses the framework you chose — maybe LangChain, maybe Semantic Kernel, maybe the Microsoft Agent Framework, maybe something entirely your own. It talks to your LLM, calls...
item.dc:creator: JamesN
